items.q: My mother is a teacher.
items.explanation: 母 + は (topic marker) + 先生 + です. は marks 母 as the topic of the sentence.

items.q: Today is Monday.
items.explanation: 今日 + は (topic) + 月よう日 + です. 今日 is a time word meaning 'today.' 毎日 means 'every day' — different from 'today.'

items.q: What day is today?
items.explanation: 今日 + は + 何よう日 + です + か. 何よう日 = 'what day of the week.' か makes the sentence a question.

items.q: My father and mother are friends.
items.explanation: 父 + と (and) + 母 + は (topic) + 友だち + です. と connects two nouns exhaustively ('father and mother').

items.q: That person is Mr. Yamamoto.
items.explanation: あの (that, over there) + 人 + は (topic) + やまもとさん + です. あの points to someone at a distance. この means 'this' — near the speaker.

items.q: It is three o'clock now.
items.explanation: 今 + は (topic) + 三時 + です. 今 means 'now.' 三時 = three o'clock.

items.q: This is 500 yen.
items.explanation: これ (this thing) + は (topic) + 五百円 + です. 五百円 = 500 yen. 千円 would be 1000 yen.

items.q: From Monday to Friday.
items.explanation: 月よう日 + から (from) + 金よう日 + まで (until). から…まで marks a range.

items.q: The test is at 10:30.
items.explanation: テスト + は (topic) + 十時半 (10:30) + です. 半 means 'half' — 十時半 = half past ten.

items.q: Every week, there is also a test.
items.explanation: 毎週 (every week) + テスト + も (also) + あります. も replaces は/が to mean 'also.' あります = exists (for inanimate things).

items.q: I go to the station.
items.explanation: わたし + は (topic) + 駅 + に (destination) + 行きます. に marks the destination with 行く. で marks where an action happens, not a destination.

items.q: A friend comes to my house.
items.explanation: 友だち + が (subject) + 家 + に (destination) + 来ます. が marks the friend as the subject. 来ます = comes (toward the speaker).

items.q: I go to the mountain by car.
items.explanation: 車 + で (by means of) + 山 + に (destination) + 行きます. で marks the means of transport. The order of 車で and 山に can be swapped.

items.q: There is a store near the station.
items.explanation: 駅 + の + ちかく (near) + に (location of existence) + 店 + が (subject) + あります. に marks where something exists. います is for living things; あります is for inanimate things like stores.

items.q: I go from the house to the station.
items.explanation: 家 + から (from) + 駅 + まで (to/until) + 行きます. から marks the starting point; まで marks the endpoint.

items.q: I eat a big lunch.
items.explanation: 大きい (big) + おひるごはん (lunch) + を (object marker) + 食べます (eat). を marks what is being eaten. 飲みます means 'drink,' not 'eat.'

items.q: I buy a new, cheap book.
items.explanation: 新しい (new) + 安い (cheap) + 本 (book) + を (object) + 買います (buy). Multiple adjectives can stack before the noun. 高い means 'expensive' — the opposite of 安い.

items.q: I drink coffee and eat bread.
items.explanation: コーヒー + を + 飲んで (te-form of 飲む) + パン + を + 食べます. Te-form (飲んで) connects two actions in sequence: 'drink [and then] eat.'

items.q: This store is not expensive.
items.explanation: この (this) + 店 + は (topic) + 高くないです (not expensive). i-adjective negative: 高い → 高くない + です.

items.q: The old book is long.
items.explanation: 古い (old) + 本 + は (topic) + 長い (long) + です. 古い modifies the noun 本 (attributive); 長い is the predicate (predicative). 新しい is the opposite of 古い.

items.q: The cat is in front of the house.
items.explanation: ねこ + は (topic) + 家 + の + 前 (front) + に (location) + います. います is for living things. あります is for inanimate things. 後ろ means 'behind.'

items.q: Today the weather is good.
items.explanation: 今日 + は (topic) + 天気 + が (subject) + いい + です. Double-は avoidance: 今日は marks the topic, then が marks 天気 as the subject within that topic. よくない is the negative form.

items.q: There is a park behind the station.
items.explanation: 駅 + の + 後ろ (behind) + に (location) + こうえん (park) + が (subject) + あります. A park is inanimate, so あります. 前 means 'front' — the opposite of 後ろ.

items.q: I am resting today.
items.explanation: 今日 + は (topic) + 休んでいます (progressive of 休む). 休んでいます = te-form 休んで + います = 'am resting' (ongoing). 休みます is simple present, not progressive.

items.q: Please turn on the light.
items.explanation: 電気 (electricity/light) + を (object) + つけてください (please turn on). Te-form + ください = polite request. けしてください means 'please turn off' — the opposite.

items.q: I study Japanese at school.
items.explanation: 学校 + で (location of action) + 日本語 + を (object) + べんきょうします. で marks where the action of studying takes place. に marks static location, not action location.

items.q: There are pretty flowers in the garden.
items.explanation: にわ (garden) + に (location of existence) + きれいな (pretty, na-adjective) + 花 + が (subject) + あります. Flowers are inanimate → あります, not います.

items.q: This book is interesting.
items.explanation: この + 本 + は (topic) + おもしろい (interesting, i-adjective) + です.

items.q: It is raining today. There are no fish.
items.explanation: 今日 + は + 雨 + です + が (but) + 魚 + は + いません (negative of います). が here means 'but,' connecting two clauses. Fish are living things → います/いません.

items.q: What country are you from?
items.explanation: どこ (where) + の + 国 (country) + から (from) + 来ました (came) + か (question). から marks the origin. 行きました means 'went' — wrong direction.

items.q: I read a book and write a letter.
items.explanation: 本 + を + 読んで (te-form of 読む) + 手がみ (letter) + を + 書きます (write). Te-form connects sequential actions.

items.q: There are many people outside.
items.explanation: 外 (outside) + に (location) + 人 + が (subject) + 多い (many) + です. 少ない (few) is the opposite of 多い.

items.q: Please speak a little slowly.
items.explanation: 少し (a little) + ゆっくり (slowly) + 話してください (please speak). たくさん means 'a lot' — the opposite of 少し.

items.q: That white building is a school.
items.explanation: あの (that, far) + 白い (white) + たてもの (building) + は (topic) + 学校 + です. この means 'this' — different distance.

items.q: I want to read many books.
items.explanation: 本 + を (object) + たくさん (many/a lot) + 読みたいです (want to read). たい-form: 読み + たいです = 'want to read.' 少し means 'a little' — opposite meaning.

items.q: Please go to the right.
items.explanation: 右 (right) + に (direction) + 行ってください (please go). 左 means 'left' — the opposite direction.

items.q: The station is to the north.
items.explanation: 駅 + は (topic) + 北 (north) + に (location) + あります. 南 means 'south' — the opposite direction.

items.q: There is a mountain to the east and a river to the west.
items.explanation: 東 + に + 山 + が + あって (te-form of ある, connecting clauses) + 西 + に + 川 + が + あります. あって connects two existence statements.

items.q: The book on top is big.
items.explanation: 上 (top/above) + の + 本 + は (topic) + 大きい + です. 上の本 = 'the book on top.' 下 means 'below' — opposite.

items.q: Please go to the left and then go south.
items.explanation: 左 + に + 行って (te-form, sequential) + 南 + に + 行ってください. Te-form connects two directions: first go left, then go south.

items.q: I enter the company at 9 AM.
items.explanation: 午前 (AM) + 九時 + に (time) + 会社 + に (destination) + 入ります (enter). 午後 means PM. 出ます means 'leave/exit' — the opposite of 入ります.

items.q: I see a friend at the entrance.
items.explanation: 入り口 (entrance) + で (location of action) + 友だち + に (target of meeting) + 会います (meet). 出口 means 'exit' — the opposite of 入り口.

items.q: My eyes are tired.
items.explanation: 目 (eyes) + が (subject) + つかれています (are tired, progressive). 耳 means 'ears' — a different body part.

items.q: Please listen with your ears and look with your eyes.
items.explanation: 耳 + で (with/using) + 聞いて (te-form of 聞く) + 目 + で (with) + 見てください. で marks the instrument/means: 'using ears' and 'using eyes.'

items.q: Please stand up and go outside.
items.explanation: 立って (te-form of 立つ, stand up) + 外 + に (destination) + 出てください (please exit). Te-form connects: 'stand up [and then] go outside.' 中 means 'inside' — the opposite of 外.
